title: Understanding Terminal Velocity
text: Terminal velocity is the constant speed that a freely falling object eventually reaches when the resistance of the medium prevents further acceleration. It can be calculated using the formula: $$v=\sqrt{\frac{2m g}{C_d p A}}$$, where $$m$$ is mass, g is acceleration due to gravity, $$C_d$$ is the drag coefficient, $$p$$ is the density of the fluid, and A is the cross-sectional area.

title: Substitute the Known Values
text: Substitute the given values into the formula: $$m=80.0$$ kg, $$g=9.81$$ $$\frac{m}{s^2}$$, $$C_d=0.70$$, $$p=1.21$$ $$\frac{kg}{m^3}$$, and $$A=0.140$$ $$m^2$$.

title: Calculate Terminal Velocity
text: Calculate the terminal velocity using the formula: $$v=\sqrt{\frac{2\times80\times9.81}{0.7\times1.21\times0.14}}$$.

title: Convert to Kilometers per Hour
text: To convert the velocity from meters per second to kilometers per hour, multiply by $$3.6$$.

title: Final Conversion
text: Convert the calculated velocity to kilometers per hour: $$56.5\times3.6$$.
